source.zh: 斯宾塞、吉伦《中澳大利亚的土著部落》（1899）、《阿兰达人》（1927）；豪伊特《东南澳大利亚的土著部落》（1904）；K·兰洛·帕克《尤瓦莱部落》（1905）；T·G·H·斯特雷洛《阿兰达传统》（1947）；拉德克利夫-布朗《澳大利亚的虹蛇神话》，《皇家人类学会志》（1926）；斯坦纳《梦创》（1953，收入《白人没有梦创》，1979）；罗纳德与凯瑟琳·伯恩特《最早的澳大利亚人的世界》（1964）；霍华德·莫菲《祖先的联结》（1991）；黛博拉·伯德·罗斯《滋养的土地》（1996）；杜安·哈马赫尔与诸长者《最早的天文学家》（2022）；乌鲁鲁-卡塔丘塔国家公园与阿南古共同发布的楚库尔帕公开讲述材料
source.en: Baldwin Spencer and F. J. Gillen, The Native Tribes of Central Australia (1899) and The Arunta (1927); A. W. Howitt, The Native Tribes of South-East Australia (1904); K. Langloh Parker, The Euahlayi Tribe (1905); T. G. H. Strehlow, Aranda Traditions (1947); A. R. Radcliffe-Brown, "The Rainbow-Serpent Myth of Australia", Journal of the Royal Anthropological Institute (1926); W. E. H. Stanner, "The Dreaming" (1953, collected in White Man Got No Dreaming, 1979); Ronald M. and Catherine H. Berndt, The World of the First Australians (1964); Howard Morphy, Ancestral Connections (1991); Deborah Bird Rose, Nourishing Terrains (1996); Duane Hamacher with Elders, The First Astronomers (2022); the public Tjukurpa material published by Anangu with Uluru-Kata Tjuta National Park

note.zh:
「澳洲原住民」不是一个民族。殖民之前这片大陆上有数百个语言群体、二百多种语言，各有各的开端叙事，彼此不构成一部共同的创世书。本页排出的这条线是编者拿各地公开材料搭的骨架，用来与其他体系对照；它不是任何一族的定本，任何一族的长者都可以说「我们不是这么讲的」，而且是对的。

「梦创时代」这个名目本身是翻译的产物。阿兰达人说 Altyerre（斯宾塞与吉伦1899年记作 Alcheringa，译成 dream times），皮坚加加拉人说 Tjukurpa，瓦尔皮里人说 Jukurrpa，约伦古人说 Wangarr，金伯利一带另有 Ngarrangkarni、Bugarrigarra。语言学者早就指出 altyerre 与「做梦」的关联在词源上并不成立，但这个译名传开了，今天原住民自己讲英语时也用它。更要紧的是时态：斯坦纳1953年造 everywhen 一词，说的是梦创没有过去时——祖先仍在他们化成的岩石与水潭里，仪式是走进梦创的路。把它排成一条从太初到当世的时间线，是为了跟本图鉴其他体系对齐，这个格式跟它讲的东西本来就相抵触，读的时候得把这一层扣掉。

材料的来路同样要摆出来。十九世纪末到二十世纪中叶的文字记录几乎全出自非原住民民族志学者：斯宾塞与吉伦（1899）、豪伊特（1904）、兰洛·帕克（1905）、斯特雷洛（1947），各带各的滤镜。豪伊特与帕克笔下的「天父」巴亚米、达拉穆伦有多少是传教接触之后的产物，学界争了一个世纪，至今没有定论。斯特雷洛发表阿兰达材料引起的争议更直接：其中一部分在当地属于只传给受戒者的知识，他印了出来，本页引用他记录的叙事时，只取长期公开流通的故事层面。虹蛇也不是一位统一的神：Rainbow Serpent 是拉德克利夫-布朗1926年提出的统称，各族的蛇各有名字、各有讲法；万吉纳只属于金伯利的沃罗拉、恩加林因、乌南巴尔三族，不通行于全大陆。

本页只写长期公开、且社区自己在教育与旅游场合对外讲述的那一层（如乌鲁鲁-卡塔丘塔国家公园与阿南古共同发布的库尼亚与利鲁叙事）。受戒者才可听的内容、仪式的具体做法、圣址的禁忌细节，一概不写。另外，这套叙事里没有「天地分离」这一格：公开材料中天空从一开始就在那儿，它本身也是一片有主的国土，不需要谁去劈开或撑起；也没有一场淹没全大陆的洪水，蒂达利克那样的旱与洪是地方性的事件。
note.en:
"Aboriginal Australia" is not one people. Before colonisation the continent held hundreds of language groups and more than 250 languages, each with its own account of how things began, and those accounts do not add up to a single book of creation. The sequence below is a scaffold the editors built from published material out of many different countries so that it can be set beside the other traditions in this atlas. It is nobody's authorised version, and an elder from any one of those nations is entitled to say "that is not how we tell it" and be right.

The phrase "the Dreaming" is itself an artefact of translation. Arrernte people say Altyerre — Spencer and Gillen wrote it Alcheringa in 1899 and glossed it "dream times" — Pitjantjatjara say Tjukurpa, Warlpiri say Jukurrpa, Yolngu say Wangarr, and the Kimberley has Ngarrangkarni and Bugarrigarra among others. Linguists have long argued that the link between altyerre and the verb "to dream" does not hold etymologically, but the English word stuck, and Aboriginal people now use it when speaking English. The harder problem is tense. Stanner coined "everywhen" in 1953 for a Dreaming that has no past tense: the ancestors are still in the rock and water they became, and ceremony is the way into it. Laying the material out as a timeline running from a beginning to a present is a concession to the format of this atlas, and the format contradicts the thing it is describing.

The provenance of the sources has to be stated as well. Almost every written record from the 1890s to the mid-twentieth century comes from non-Aboriginal ethnographers — Spencer and Gillen (1899), Howitt (1904), Langloh Parker (1905), Strehlow (1947) — each with his own filter. How much of the "All-Father" figure that Howitt and Parker describe, Baiame and Daramulun, took its shape after missionary contact has been argued over for a century and is still not settled. The Strehlow case is sharper: some of the Aranda material he published is knowledge that, in its own country, passes only to initiated men, and he printed it. Where his records are used here, only the openly circulated narrative layer is drawn on. The Rainbow Serpent, likewise, is not one god: the term is Radcliffe-Brown's umbrella coinage of 1926, and each people's serpent has its own name and its own story. The Wandjina belong to the Worrorra, Ngarinyin and Wunambal of the Kimberley, and to no one else.

What follows keeps to the layer that has long been public and that communities themselves tell to outsiders in schools and on country — for instance the Kuniya and Liru narrative published by Anangu with Uluru-Kata Tjuta National Park. Nothing restricted to initiates is included: no ceremonial procedure, no site prohibitions, no material held back for those with the standing to hear it. Two absences are worth naming. There is no separation of sky and earth in this material: the sky is simply there from the start, and it is country too, with owners of its own; nothing has to be split or propped up. And there is no flood that drowns the continent — droughts and floods like Tiddalik's are local events with local consequences.

title.zh: 一片认不出地方的平地
title.en: A flat plain with no places in it

text.zh: 各族讲法不同，开头却常是差不多的一句：大地是一片没有起伏的平原，没有山、没有河，也没有一处能认出来、叫得出名字的地方。斯特雷洛1947年在《阿兰达传统》里发表的叙事说，那时黑暗压在地上，像一层拨不开的灌木丛；祖先存在就睡在这层地皮底下。伊尔巴林查的袋狸祖先卡罗拉躺在泉眼下面的土里，上方立着一根活的柱子，柱身覆着像人一样的皮肤。
text.en: Tellings differ from country to country, but they tend to open the same way. The first earth was a flat plain: no ranges, no rivers, no place that could be named or told apart from any other. In the Arrernte account T. G. H. Strehlow published in Aranda Traditions in 1947, darkness lay over the ground like a thicket nothing could push through, and the ancestral beings slept beneath the crust of it. At Ilbalintja the bandicoot ancestor Karora lay asleep in the soil below the soak, a living pole swaying above him, its skin like the skin of a man.

text.zh: 袋狸先从卡罗拉的肚脐和腋窝里钻出来，顶开地皮；太阳这才第一次升起，卡罗拉自己破土而起，他躺过的地方塌成一个坑，就是伊尔巴林查的泉。别处的祖先来路不同：巨蛇从常年不涸的深潭底下钻上来，金伯利的万吉纳自云与海中现身，东南部的巴亚米从天上下来。它们有名有姓：袋鼠、鸸鹋、蟒蛇、老鹰、七个姐妹，会打猎，会打架，也会做错事。
text.en: Bandicoots came out of Karora's navel and his armpits first and burst through the sod above him; only then did the sun rise for the first time and Karora himself break through the crust, and the hollow where he had lain became the soak at Ilbalintja. Elsewhere the ancestors arrive by other roads. Great serpents come up from the bottom of waterholes that never dry. The Wandjina of the Kimberley come out of the clouds and the sea. Baiame comes down from the sky in the south-east. They are named beings — kangaroo, emu, python, eagle, seven sisters — who hunt, quarrel, and get things wrong.

title.zh: 走过的地方才有了形状
title.en: Country takes its shape behind them

text.zh: 祖先一走动，地就有了形状：脊背在地下拱起来是山岭，爬行的沟成了河道，歇脚的地方陷下去积成水潭。西南部的努恩加人说，天鹅河就是瓦格尔蜿蜒爬出来的一条路。阿南古人读乌鲁鲁的岩面：一队利鲁毒蛇武士杀了沃玛蟒女库尼亚的侄儿，库尼亚在穆提朱鲁水潭边与为首的一个对决，掘杖两次砸在他头上，岩壁上两道深长的裂槽就是当时的杖痕。走过的路连成歌之路，把歌按次序唱下去，就是把这条路走一遍；一条路能横穿半个大陆，沿途每一段由归属那一段的人分头照看。
text.en: As the ancestors travelled, the country took its shape behind them: a back arching underground became a ridge, a track gouged out became a riverbed, a resting place sank and filled as a waterhole. The Noongar say the Swan River is the winding road the Wagyl cut for itself. Anangu read the rock at Mutitjulu as the record of a fight: a band of Liru, venom-snake warriors, killed the nephew of Kuniya the woma python woman, and she took on their leader there and brought her digging stick down twice on his head — the two long gouges in the face of the rock are where the blows landed. The routes the ancestors walked became songlines: sing the verses in their order and you are walking the road. A single line can cross half the continent, and each stretch of it is looked after by the people who belong to that stretch.

title.zh: 石刀刻出手指、嘴和眼睛
title.en: Stone knives cut fingers, mouths, eyes

text.zh: 人从哪儿来，各族的答案对不上，很多讲法里人根本不用造，祖先自己就是人的来处。斯宾塞与吉伦1899年记下的阿兰达讲法则很具体：两个「无中而出」的自存者乌甘比库拉，遇上一批未成形的生物因纳佩尔特瓦——蜷成一团，没有四肢，没有五官。他们用石刀一刀刀削：先分出手臂和手指，再是腿和脚，然后开出眼睛、嘴和鼻孔，刻成男人和女人，分别归入袋鼠、鸸鹋等图腾。活干完，乌甘比库拉把自己变成了小蜥蜴。西阿纳姆地的比宁人另有一说：先民到的时候，米米灵早已在岩壁上作画，是米米灵教会他们用火、剥解袋鼠、把颜料上到石头上。
text.en: Where people came from is answered differently from country to country, and in many tellings people need no making at all, the ancestors themselves being where people come from. The Arrernte version Spencer and Gillen recorded in 1899 is unusually concrete. Two self-existing beings called Ungambikula, a name glossed "out of nothing", came upon the Inapertwa: half-made creatures huddled in a heap, without limbs or features. They cut them into shape with stone knives — arms and fingers freed first, then legs and feet, then the eyes, the mouth and the nostrils opened — and the men and women that came out of that work belonged to the kangaroo, the emu and the other totems. The job done, the Ungambikula turned themselves into small lizards. In western Arnhem Land the Bininj tell it differently: the Mimih were already painting on the rock when the first people arrived, and it was the Mimih who taught them fire, how to butcher a kangaroo, and how to get paint onto stone.

title.en: The frog who drank the water

text.zh: 劫是地方性的，没有一场淹掉整个大陆的洪水。维多利亚吉普斯兰的贡艾库尔奈人讲蒂达利克：巨蛙一口气把河、湖、溪、泉全喝干，草木枯死，众兽渴得走不动路，只剩下一个法子——轮流到它面前出丑，逗它笑。流传最广的讲法里，是鳗鱼诺云努姆离了水，在滚烫的干泥上扭成一串结，巨蛙终于笑出了声，水从它嘴里奔涌而出，河湖泉眼重新灌满。破规矩另有报应：西阿纳姆地的恩加约德吞下惊扰圣地的人，再把他们呕成岩石与地景；雷季里闪电之灵纳玛尔贡高坐积雨云顶，谁在雷暴底下喧哗、嘲弄雷声，他就朝谁掷下石斧。
text.en: Catastrophe here is local; no flood drowns the continent. The Gunaikurnai of Gippsland tell of Tiddalik, the frog who drank every river, lake, creek and soak dry, until the plants withered and the animals were too parched to walk and could think of nothing to do but take turns making fools of themselves in front of him to get him to laugh. In the best-known telling the eel Nabunum, out of the water, writhes and knots itself on the baked mud, the frog laughs out loud at last, and the water bursts out of him and runs back into the rivers, the lakes and the soaks. Breaking the Law has its own consequences: in western Arnhem Land Ngalyod swallows those who disturb a sacred place and vomits them back out as rock and landscape, and in the wet season the lightning spirit Namarrkon, seated on the thunderheads, throws his stone axes at whoever shouts under a storm or mocks the thunder.

title.zh: 祖先做过一遍，后人照着做
title.en: Done once, and done that way ever since

text.zh: 祖先留下的不只是地形，还有规矩：谁能跟谁结婚，哪一片地归谁照看，什么东西不能吃、不能碰、不能提，仪式该怎么办。这套东西统称「律法」（Law）：祖先怎么做过一遍，后人就怎么做，没有哪一位神颁下过条文。语言也是这样留下的——走到哪儿，就把哪一段话留在哪儿，所以语言的边界常常跟着地形和祖先的路走。东南部的巴亚米据说立下了成年礼博拉的规程，办完便回了天上；猎人谷米尔布罗代尔的岩窟里，画着他双臂大张的形象。
text.en: The ancestors left more than the shape of the ground. They left the rules: who may marry whom, which family answers for which stretch of country, what may not be eaten or touched or spoken of, how a ceremony is to be run. All of it is called the Law: what the ancestors did once is what is done now, and no one handed down a code. Language was laid down the same way, each stretch of a track carrying the words that belong to it, which is why language boundaries so often run with the country and with the ancestors' roads. In the south-east, Baiame is said to have set out the bora, the initiation ground and its order, and to have gone back up to the sky when it was finished; in a rock shelter at Milbrodale in the Hunter Valley he is painted with his arms flung wide.

text.zh: 事情做完，祖先躺回大地，或升上夜空，但都没有离开。金伯利的万吉纳把自己的形象躺进岩壁，力量留在画里；旱季末尾，有资格的长者为画补色描新，雨才会按时到来。昆卡兰卡帕七姐妹被违犯婚律的男人瓦提·尼鲁一路追过西部沙漠，追到海边无路可走，纵身跃上夜空成了昴星团；尼鲁追上天去，成了猎户座那几颗亮星，一夜又一夜地跟在后头。天上的鸸鹋由银河里的暗尘带勾出来：南十字座旁的煤袋星云是它的头，暗带一路拖出脖颈和身子，它随季节换姿势，告诉人什么时候该去捡鸸鹋蛋。祖先仍在他们化成的石头和水潭里：每个人经由出生地和亲属关系继承特定的梦创，一并继承照看那片地的责任。斯坦纳为这个时态造了个词，everywhen。
text.en: Their work finished, the ancestors lay down into the land or rose into the sky, and neither move took them away. The Wandjina laid their images into the rock of the Kimberley and left their power in the paint; at the end of the dry season authorised elders refresh the paintings, and that is what brings the rain on time. The Seven Sisters, Kungkarangkalpa, were chased across the Western Desert by Wati Nyiru, a man pursuing them against the marriage Law, and when the sea cut off their road they leapt into the sky as the Pleiades, with Nyiru behind them in the bright stars of Orion, still following, night after night. The emu overhead is a dark constellation: its head is the Coalsack beside the Southern Cross, its neck and body are the dust lanes running down the Milky Way, and the way it lies at a given season tells people when to go looking for emu eggs. The ancestors remain in the rock and the water they became. A person inherits particular Dreamings through birthplace and kin, and inherits with them the duty to look after that country. Stanner's word for this tense was everywhen.
